findings.finding: Step 1 retargeting from state.features to ctx["state"]["features"] is covered only by render smoke test; no fixture-driven test exercises the round-filter against representative state.
findings.reviewer_angle: Mechanical retargeting silently breaks orchestrator semantics
findings.evidence_quote: if pseudocode uses state.features the orchestrator now reads ctx["state"]["features"] (same dict shape as asdict(OvernightState)).

findings.finding: No fixture-driven semantic test asserts round-spawn decisions match pre-rewrite for representative inputs; verification is grep + render smoke test only.
findings.reviewer_angle: Mechanical retargeting silently breaks orchestrator semantics
findings.evidence_quote: just test tests/test_orchestrator_prompt_render.py exits 0 — pass if exit 0 (prompt still renders with substituted variables).

findings.finding: Conflict-recovery no-op assumption is conditional on an unresolved if; lines 200-212 are prose only (assumption holds), but the plan did not document the inspection.
findings.reviewer_angle: Mechanical retargeting silently breaks orchestrator semantics
findings.evidence_quote: Step 1b conflict-recovery awareness (lines 200-212) — if the prompt currently does not read overnight-events.log inline, retargeting is a no-op for this section

findings.finding: R11 verification (grep for baseline_tokens: presence) does not bind the captured integer to actual measurement; carries no evidentiary weight when R12 reads it.
findings.reviewer_angle: Baseline-capture gating is impractical and unenforced
findings.evidence_quote: Interactive/session-dependent: the value of baseline_tokens comes from running ticket 104 aggregator against a real overnight session — no synthetic command can produce it. The grep above only confirms the field is present.

findings.finding: Task 1 implements non-trivial overnight-events.log streaming/parsing/filtering for merge_conflict_events that no current Task 5 prompt rewrite consumes.
findings.evidence_quote: overnight-events.log — stream line-by-line, json.loads, filter event == merge_conflict_classified; missing/unreadable -> [].

findings.finding: R10 strict-set contract assertion pins merge_conflict_events as load-bearing in the test schema even though no production code reads it; removal later requires schema bump + test rewrite.
findings.evidence_quote: assert set(result.keys()) == {schema_version, ..., merge_conflict_events}. Adding any new top-level key without bumping the test set breaks this

findings.finding: R8 RuntimeError is structurally unreachable in production — both literals are co-located in the same module and bumped together; mechanism rarely fires unless single-edit oversight.
findings.reviewer_angle: Schema-version drift catches narrow class of bugs and tests tautologically
findings.evidence_quote: define _EXPECTED_SCHEMA_VERSION = 1 as a module-level constant. Construct the dict with literal schema_version: 1.

findings.finding: R8 unit test monkeypatches the very condition production code makes structurally impossible — proves raise statement is reachable, not that any real bug class is caught.
findings.reviewer_angle: Schema-version drift catches narrow class of bugs and tests tautologically
findings.evidence_quote: monkeypatch the dict-construction site (or directly mutate _EXPECTED_SCHEMA_VERSION) so payload[schema_version] differs from the constant; assert RuntimeError raised
